mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and falling consumer spending power, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses the threshold, or the signal loses predictive power during recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a broad contraction in consumer demand and credit-dependent spending, which compresses corporate revenue forecasts and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative 20-day returns, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in the index of industrial production reflect strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which triggers short-term momentum buying and positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns during periods of high inflation, or the signal reverses direction when CPIAUCSL exceeds 4.0% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained acceleration in consumer price inflation erodes real corporate margins and forces monetary tightening expectations, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive when CPIAUCSL exceeds the threshold, or the signal shows no predictive power in low-growth environments.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly increases in total nonfarm payrolls indicate expanding economic activity and rising household income, which supports consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero correlation with 20-day forward returns over a 5-year sample, or Rank IC falls below 0.01 consistently.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product confirms sustained aggregate demand expansion, which improves corporate earnings trajectories and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns during periods of financial stress, or the signal loses significance when credit spreads widen beyond 200 basis points.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in industrial production and payroll employment signal synchronized supply-side and labor market strength, which triggers algorithmic momentum strategies and positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform baseline returns, or the interaction effect vanishes when monetary policy shifts to aggressive tightening.
